heading: Requirements relating to National Academy of Sciences and National Academy of Public Administration
label: 1014 Requirements relating to National Academy of Sciences and National Academy of Public Administration

children.text: the committee was not subject to any actual management or control by an agency or an officer of the Federal Government;
in the case of a committee created after , the membership of the committee was appointed in accordance with the requirements described in subsection (b)(1); andDecember 17, 1997

children.label: A
children.text: subsection (b)(2) through (6), in the case of any advice or recommendation provided by the National Academy of Sciences; or

children.label: B
children.text: subsection (b)(2) and (5), in the case of any advice or recommendation provided by the National Academy of Public Administration.

children.label: A
children.text: no individual appointed to serve on the committee has a conflict of interest that is relevant to the functions to be performed, unless such conflict is promptly and publicly disclosed and the Academy determines that the conflict is unavoidable;

children.label: B
children.text: the committee membership is fairly balanced as determined by the Academy to be appropriate for the functions to be performed; and

children.label: C
children.text: the final report of the Academy will be the result of the Academy’s independent judgment.

children.heading: .—Public notice of committee meetings
children.label: 2 .—Public notice of committee meetings
children.text: The Academy shall determine and provide public notice of committee meetings that will be open to the public.

children.heading: .—Data-gathering meetings
children.label: 3 .—Data-gathering meetings
children.text: The Academy shall ensure that meetings of the committee to gather data from individuals who are not officials, agents, or employees of the Academy are open to the public, unless the Academy determines that a meeting would disclose matters described in . The Academy shall make available to the public, at reasonable charge if appropriate, written materials presented to the committee by individuals who are not officials, agents, or employees of the Academy, unless the Academy determines that making material available would disclose matters described in .section 552(b) of this titlesection 552(b) of this title

children.heading: .—Other meetings
children.label: 4 .—Other meetings
children.text: The Academy shall make available to the public as soon as practicable, at reasonable charge if appropriate, a brief summary of any committee meeting that is not a data-gathering meeting, unless the Academy determines that the summary would disclose matters described in . The summary shall identify the committee members present, the topics discussed, materials made available to the committee, and other matters the Academy determines should be included.section 552(b) of this title

children.heading: .—Final report
children.label: 5 .—Final report
children.text: The Academy shall make available to the public its final report, at reasonable charge if appropriate, unless the Academy determines that the report would disclose matters described in . If the Academy determines that the report would disclose matters described in , the Academy shall make public an abbreviated version of the report that does not disclose those matters.section 552(b) of this titlesection 552(b) of this title

children.heading: .—Reviewers of report
children.label: 6 .—Reviewers of report
children.text: After publication of the final report, the Academy shall make publicly available the names of the principal reviewers who reviewed the report in draft form and who are not officials, agents, or employees of the Academy.

children.heading: .—Regulations
children.label: c .—Regulations
children.text: The Administrator of General Services may issue regulations implementing this section.
